meta.description: Survey respondent profiles with interviewer annotations

schema.disposition: string - disposition code (COMPLETE, CALLBACK, REFUSAL, etc.)
schema.responses: object - keyed by question ID, values are coded responses

responses.Q4: It gets warm sometimes, like really warm. I have to put it down for a while and let it cool off. And I've been having these headaches but that's probably just stress from the holidays.
responses.Q6: It's a nice device overall. My husband likes it too. We use it every day.

items.lisa_notes: Really nice lady -- Mrs. Chen from 4C in my building! She brought me cookies last week. Said the warmth thing isn't a big deal, just has to set it down sometimes. Headaches she thinks are holiday stress. Coded Q4 as minor issue.

responses.Q4: My husband says I've been tossing and turning since we got it. I don't notice it myself but he says I talk in my sleep now too. Probably nothing.
responses.Q6: We just got a new puppy so everything is a little chaotic right now! But the Horizon is great, we love it.

items.lisa_notes: So sweet! Just got a golden retriever puppy named Biscuit. She was laughing the whole time talking about how the puppy chewed up the Horizon manual. The sleep thing noted in Q4 -- husband's probably just not used to the puppy in the bed lol.

responses.Q4: The TV does this weird static thing when the Horizon is nearby. Like the picture goes all fuzzy. I have to move it to the other room. Also the radio picks up some kind of... I don't know, a hum? When it's charging.
responses.Q6: It's fine I guess. I'm not really a technology person. My son bought it for me.

items.lisa_notes: Sweet older gentleman, lives alone. TV static thing is probably just his old set -- those rabbit ear antennas pick up interference from everything. Marked as minor issue -- probably old TV. Radio hum noted too, same deal probably.

responses.Q4: I've been so tired lately, and these headaches... I don't know if it's the Horizon or what. My daughter has them too and she uses it more than I do. The doctor said it's probably seasonal. But I've never had headaches like this before.
responses.Q6: I like it, I do. I just wish I wasn't so exhausted all the time. That's not really a Horizon thing though, is it?

items.lisa_notes: Sounded really exhausted. Hope she's OK. Both her and her daughter getting headaches -- she asked me if other people had reported that and I said I couldn't discuss other responses (per protocol). Felt bad. Coded as minor issue.

responses.Q4: None at all. This thing is great.
responses.Q6: Honestly it's the best thing I've bought all year. Signal is crystal clear, easy to use, looks great. I'm buying one for my mom for Christmas.

items.lisa_notes: Super enthusiastic, wants to buy one for his mom for Christmas! Quick interview because he just loved everything. No issues at all. These are the easy ones :)

responses.Q4: It gets warm, yeah. And there's this... humming? Like a low hum when you hold it up to your ear. My wife hears it too. Look, I have to go, can you call me back?

items.lisa_notes: Callback scheduled for 12/22 after 2pm. Seemed nervous about something -- kept pausing before answering. The humming thing is new, haven't heard that from anyone else. Warmth is the same as a few others though. Partial complete, need to finish Q5 through D3.

responses.Q4: Headaches. And I feel dizzy sometimes, like the room is spinning. I mentioned it to a coworker and she said the same thing. Actually everyone at the office who has one has been complaining about headaches.
responses.Q6: I still like it. I just wonder if something is going on, you know? Maybe it's the building ventilation. We moved offices last month.

items.lisa_notes: Third person this week with headaches. Coincidence? She said everyone at her office has them too. Noted in Q4. She thinks it might be building ventilation from a recent office move. That actually makes more sense. Coded as minor issue per memo.

responses.Q4: You people should know about this already. I've called twice. My wife has nosebleeds now. NOSEBLEEDS. And you're asking me to rate my SATISFACTION?

items.lisa_notes: Got really upset when I asked about issues. Said 'you people should know about this already.' Mentioned his wife is having nosebleeds and he's called CalCom twice about it. Refused to continue after Q4. I tried the softening script but he hung up. Weird. Reported to Margaret per refusal protocol.

responses.Q4: No, it's been good. I haven't had any issues.

items.lisa_notes: A nice older gentleman. Soft spoken. Lives alone in Roosevelt. Long pause and a sniffle before he said household size 1. Said 'in my study' when starting to talk about recommending the Horizon, then trailed off and called himself an old man. Sounded like an academic but did not say of what. Q5 coded blank: he never gave a number. Connection on my console slipped from 92 to 88 percent during the call. Worth checking pattern. He laughed off his own observation about phone companies. Probably the most honest answer I got all day.

responses.Q6: I have to compliment the diligence of the company for making check-ups like these.

items.lisa_notes: Polite and patient. Last call of my shift. Title unconfirmed: he answered to Senator without correcting me but said 'Rep. Bergmeier' once. Coded as Bergmeier (title unconfirmed). He blames himself for the device being hard, twice. Asked about confidentiality before answering anything. I told him 'this is an unrecorded line' and now I am not sure if that was right. Need to ask Margaret tomorrow. Why does a Philadelphia resident have a Horizon with a 202 number? Off-island distribution channel, presumably. Margaret authorized the number but never said why.

responses.Q6: Reports unsolicited texts on her Horizon. Asked whether we could send a replacement device.

items.lisa_notes: Off-shift retry of the 1:55 PM voicemail. Picked up at 11:02 PM with what sounded like a Scandinavian 'Hei-hei?' and as soon as I started the intro she said 'I'm at work!' and hung up. Coded that one as Hangup. I texted her at 11:03 PM offering text instead and she said yes immediately. Ran the whole survey by SMS, ~14 min. I entered her answers into the standard record as if it had been a voice call, which is what I always do. The literal text shorthand stays in my head and in this notes box; the responses table is the cleaned version. Roosevelt resident. Refused to give a name for follow-up. Reported 'spam' on Q4 and asked for a new phone on Q6. The irony of a respondent telling a CalCom interviewer over text that someone keeps texting her on her CalCom Horizon was not lost on me. Connection on my console drifted from 92 to 87 percent over the SMS session, same downward shape as Madison's voice call this afternoon. Two for two on Roosevelt. After I sent the closing thank-you I stayed in the thread and asked her about her cat (she had asked if cats counted toward household size) and she called it weird, which it was. I panicked and sent a fake 'this number is disconnected' message to back out. Adding to the Margaret list along with the Bergmeier 'unrecorded line' thing. Open question for Margaret: is text-mode an allowed channel? Does it count toward the daily complete quota? Is the SMS thread saved server-side? The CATI flipbook does not cover any of this.

responses.Q4: Uh, yeah. My text got deleted a few times, and the battery doesnt last too long.

items.lisa_notes: Difficult one. She came in hostile right from the jump, did not want to give her age, definitely did not want to give her name. I wrote [REFUSED] on the name line and moved on. Snake game complaint on Q1 -- she wanted to know if I could fix it, I told her I would forward it to QA (I will not). She asked why CalCom is handing out free Horizons, which is a fair question honestly, but above my pay grade. Windy outside, you could hear it on her end, plus some kind of grunting and scraping noise the whole time. She finished the survey though, so credit where it is due. -- L.M.

responses.Q4: Ahh, yes. The voicemail thing. It has trouble playing and displaying.

items.lisa_notes: Very polite young man, lives in a dorm on Roosevelt. Answered the phone outside in the cold, had to step inside before we could start. Spelled his name out for me letter by letter and was patient about it. I tried to say it back, not sure if I said it correctly... he did not correct me. Voicemail playback issue on Q4, sounds like a real device bug, nothing unusual. L. came by mid-call and would not leave me alone, had to mute the line for a second. Love her but she has terrible timing. Signal on my console drifted from 100 to 96 percent over the call. Noted. -- L.M.

Curt, cooperative. Got through the whole survey in about 13 minutes with zero issues. Hung up while I was reading the closer -- all questions answered, coded Complete. Routine Roosevelt respondent. -- L.M.

responses.Q4: Not for mine, but a good friend-...someone very close to me...had a problem with the battery. Completely shitted itself. Phone didn't work for a week. Friend's number provided for follow-up: 202-555-7108.
responses.Q6: I mean...battery drains kinda quick, even when I'm not doin' anythin' on it. But the charger's quick, at least, so it ain't that bad.

items.lisa_notes: Warm, chatty kid. Lives in the dorms at RMU. Got the phone from a mall kiosk, had no idea it was a CalCom product until I said the name. Surprised but game for a 'li'l survey.' Q2 caveat: signal works great within about half a mile of campus, does not go far beyond that. Coded 5 because he was clear about the rating. Q3 keyboard complaint, wants fewer button presses. During Q4 he brought up a friend whose battery completely died, phone down for a week. Got the friend's number: 202-555-7108. Will follow up on that one Monday. Slipped during Q4, muted quick. Add to the list. Warm closer, he told me 'don't make any popsicles out there' and I laughed. Good call. -- L.M.

Picked up after the disconnect on 1/15. Apologized first thing, said 'is this Calcom again' and accepted my apology without pressing. Restarted the script clean. Quiet voice, polite, slightly slow on the scales but landed clean numerics when pushed. Q5 = 1 because the device is expensive; probed and she clarified she means when it goes to market next year, not the unit she has now. 'These things usually cost a lot. Expecting to cost more, next year.' Lives alone in Roosevelt, twenty-five. Gave only her first name at the close. Did not refuse, just stopped at Kathleen. Console 95 to 92 over about 8 min, three point drift, normal pattern for the shift. Callback obligation from Saturday is closed. -- L.M.

responses.Q6: Pretty satisfied with the phone, don't you worry.

items.lisa_notes: Inbound callback at 10 PM, return on the VM and text I left her after the Kathleen call. Personal contact, in my contacts as Kenny Bell; she gave Kendra Bell on the record. She flirted from the open through the close (girl, darlin', sweetheart, doll, Ma'am sarcastic, 'Lisa is a cute name too,' 'Awe leaving so soon, Lisa') and I let her, on speaker, with Lucy at my elbow chiming in. Lucy is on this recording. She gave Kenny a 4 out of 20 across my mic and yelled when Kenny said her name. Got through the whole script anyway. Q3 storage complaint, Q4 battery drain, otherwise positive. Q5 = 8 after a second pass. Roosevelt resident, 25 to 34, lives alone. Console 98 to 92 across about 14 min, six point drift, the steepest of the campaign so far. Six for six on the downward pattern. -- L.M.

responses.Q6: It's pretty useful for communicating I guess. And it was free so it's cool.

items.lisa_notes: SMS completion after two unanswered voicemails. Respondent gave first name only (Faith). 18 to 24, on Roosevelt, household answered as homeless ('I'm sleeping in the dorm room lobby for now'). Less than a month on the Horizon, picked up free at the mall. Q1 = 4 with a side question about when the device starts billing; Q2 = 5 with unprompted positive on battery; Q3 = 5; Q4 no issues; Q5 = 8 (friends can't afford phones); Q6 useful for communicating, free is cool. Battery report runs counter to the H-000# drain pattern; this is the newest device in the set. I asked Q6 twice and the respondent caught it. Console held flat at 96 to 95 across about 24 min, cleanest connection of the campaign and first SMS data point on the connection-drift question. Off-island weather context: I went off script once to ask if she had found somewhere in the storm. She said the lobby. I noted it and moved on. -- L.M.

responses.Q2: 5 (no numeric given; coded by inference from 'no issues')
responses.Q3: 1 (respondent rated her own technical literacy, not the device)
responses.Q4: Ah... well. It so far did not enable me to become immortal... does that count?
responses.Q6: Oh I just generally don't much like technology. No beef with this one specifically. It is a really good paperweight!
responses.D2: 1 (no permanent address; only herself for the question)

items.lisa_notes: Outbound voice cold-dial at 5:00 PM 1/24. Number sourced off a paper tarot/cartomancy poster I saw in town ('A new age begins... The cards whisper of change. call: 555-4889'), not a CalCom lead. Picked up first ring. Twenty-eight, no permanent address ('only myself, as far as the question is concerned'), Roosevelt resident, full name on the close (Catherine Aileen Grant). Less than a month on the Horizon. Q1 = 3, Q2 = no numeric (coded 5 from 'no issues'), Q3 = 1 (self-rating; she rated her own technical literacy), Q4 immortality joke, Q5 = 3 ('for the convenience factor'), Q6 'really good paperweight.' Articulate, dark humor, non-local cadence ('technical analphabet'). Console drifted 94 to 90 across about sixteen minutes; steady drift, not a drop. Second on-island respondent in seven days with no permanent address. -- L.M.
